title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Engineering with Python 3.14
description: Python developers who can design and test ordinary object-oriented programs and need to build, evaluate, secure, operate, and defend evidence-grounded retrieval-augmented systems from first principles through production release. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; information-retrieval foundations; inverted indexes; unicode and multilingual preprocessing; provenance-preserving parsing and ocr; chunking; tf-idf and bm25; relevance judgments and ranked metrics; embeddings and vector similarity; dense retrieval; exact and approximate indexes; vector-store lifecycle; hybrid fusion; query rewriting and decomposition; cross-encoder and late-interaction reranking; context compression and position; grounded generation and abstention; accessible citations; versioned pipeline architecture; component and end-to-end evaluation; evaluation datasets; rag security, privacy, governance, observability, performance, continuous ingestion, disaster recovery, structured and graphrag, adaptive and agentic rag, multimodal rag, architecture evolution, testing, release, rollback, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python models using fixed fixtures plus explicit python 3.14.6, numpy 2.5.1, scikit-learn 1.9.0, sentence-transformers 5.6.0, faiss 1.14.3, pgvector 0.8.2, evaluated embedding and generation models, vector database, ocr, provider, network, load, fault, privacy, security, accessibility, restore, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic evidence-grounded systems and a performance-based production defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Python correctness, source and corpus identity, document and chunk lineage, query and relevance assumptions, tenant authorization, freshness and deletion policy, bounded work, accessible status, untrusted-data separation, changed-case evaluation, safe abstention, causal failure evidence, and explicit browser-versus-production limits before adding one RAG boundary.

Browser Python is deterministic and isolated. It models ingestion ledgers, token streams, chunks, lexical and vector scores, indexes, filters, fusion, reranking, context, claims, citations, evaluations, policy, traces, cost, state, and recovery using fixed fixtures. It never loads real models, imports third-party ML packages, opens files or networks, contacts databases or providers, uses credentials, executes commands, or causes external effects.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, versioned corpus and components, a prediction, inspectable intermediate stage evidence, changed query and corpus cases, at least one rejected or abstained case, accessible claim-level sources, independent retrieval and generation evaluation, failure and recovery evidence, and named native or production transfer gates.

competencies.statement: Define the user, decision, information need, authoritative sources, freshness need, consequence of error, and observable outcome before choosing RAG.
misconceptions: A conversational interface is itself a measurable RAG outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define the user, decision, information need, authoritative sources, freshness need, consequence of error, and observable outcome before choosing RAG.

competencies.statement: Specify answerable, unanswerable, ambiguous, unauthorized, stale, and conflicting cases with a useful abstention and escalation contract.
misconceptions: A production assistant should always answer because silence is worse than uncertainty.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify answerable, unanswerable, ambiguous, unauthorized, stale, and conflicting cases with a useful abstention and escalation contract.

competencies.statement: Separate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, system, and stakeholder evidence so one passing stage cannot hide another failure.
misconceptions: A plausible final answer proves that retrieval worked.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, system, and stakeholder evidence so one passing stage cannot hide another failure.

competencies.statement: Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.
misconceptions: The most complex RAG architecture is the safest starting baseline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish searchable-document, lexical, and long-context baselines before adding embeddings, rerankers, agents, or graphs.

competencies.statement: Bound corpus use, evaluation, monitoring, and red teaming by authorization, licensing, privacy, accessibility, domain expertise, and incident duties.
misconceptions: Publicly reachable content is automatically authorized for ingestion and redistribution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound corpus use, evaluation, monitoring, and red teaming by authorization, licensing, privacy, accessibility, domain expertise, and incident duties.

competencies.statement: Model corpus, document, field, query, information need, relevance, judgment, candidate, rank, and cutoff as distinct objects.
misconceptions: Relevance is an inherent property of a document independent of a query and user.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model corpus, document, field, query, information need, relevance, judgment, candidate, rank, and cutoff as distinct objects.

competencies.statement: Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.
misconceptions: Boolean retrieval automatically ranks the most useful matching document first.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.

competencies.statement: Build term dictionaries and postings with document identity, frequency, position, field, and deterministic serialization evidence.
misconceptions: An inverted index stores one complete document under every term.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build term dictionaries and postings with document identity, frequency, position, field, and deterministic serialization evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain which order, syntax, negation, scope, and discourse evidence bag-of-words models discard.
misconceptions: A bag-of-words representation preserves sentence meaning because it preserves every token.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain which order, syntax, negation, scope, and discourse evidence bag-of-words models discard.

competencies.statement: Define stable score, tie-break, pagination, and snapshot rules so repeated retrieval is reproducible.
misconceptions: Equal floating-point scores can be returned in any order without affecting tests or users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define stable score, tie-break, pagination, and snapshot rules so repeated retrieval is reproducible.

competencies.statement: Inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document.
misconceptions: A filename and upload date are complete source provenance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document.

competencies.statement: Parse HTML, Markdown, PDF, office, JSON, email, and plain text through format-specific admission, size, recursion, and error contracts.
misconceptions: Extracted plain text proves every source format was parsed correctly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse HTML, Markdown, PDF, office, JSON, email, and plain text through format-specific admission, size, recursion, and error contracts.

competencies.statement: Retain page, region, reading order, table, figure, coordinate, OCR confidence, and source-image links when extraction needs layout evidence.
misconceptions: High OCR character accuracy guarantees correct tables and reading order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain page, region, reading order, table, figure, coordinate, OCR confidence, and source-image links when extraction needs layout evidence.

competencies.statement: Detect exact and near duplicates while preserving editions, amendments, canonical identity, supersession, and provenance.
misconceptions: Documents with similar text are interchangeable duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect exact and near duplicates while preserving editions, amendments, canonical identity, supersession, and provenance.

competencies.statement: Quarantine malformed, unauthorized, poisoned, executable, hidden, or policy-violating content and always treat accepted content as untrusted data.
misconceptions: Content becomes trusted instruction after it passes malware scanning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quarantine malformed, unauthorized, poisoned, executable, hidden, or policy-violating content and always treat accepted content as untrusted data.

competencies.statement: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
misconceptions: NFKC is harmless for every natural-language and identifier field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.

competencies.statement: Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
misconceptions: Whitespace tokenization works equally well for every writing system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.

competencies.statement: Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.
misconceptions: Concatenating every field into one string preserves structure and policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.

competencies.statement: Evaluate case folding, stemming, lemmatization, stop words, synonyms, and accent handling by language, domain, and changed query.
misconceptions: Removing common words always improves retrieval quality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate case folding, stemming, lemmatization, stop words, synonyms, and accent handling by language, domain, and changed query.

competencies.statement: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.
misconceptions: A chunk identifier is enough to reconstruct an exact human-inspectable citation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.

competencies.statement: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
misconceptions: One universal chunk size is optimal for every document and query.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.

competencies.statement: Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.
misconceptions: Maximum overlap increases information without adding retrieval duplicates or cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.

competencies.statement: Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.
misconceptions: Chunk body text alone contains every signal needed for retrieval and interpretation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.

competencies.statement: Retrieve small evidence units while expanding bounded parents, neighbors, tables, or sections without crossing document or access boundaries.
misconceptions: Parent-document expansion is safe even when child and parent permissions differ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retrieve small evidence units while expanding bounded parents, neighbors, tables, or sections without crossing document or access boundaries.

competencies.statement: Derive stable document and chunk identity from source, version, structure, offsets, and content so updates, deletes, caches, and citations reconcile.
misconceptions: Random chunk IDs are sufficient for incremental indexing and deletion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive stable document and chunk identity from source, version, structure, offsets, and content so updates, deletes, caches, and citations reconcile.

competencies.statement: Compute term and document frequency from a declared token stream and explain saturation, repetition, and field effects.
misconceptions: Repeating a query term indefinitely should increase relevance linearly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute term and document frequency from a declared token stream and explain saturation, repetition, and field effects.

competencies.statement: Implement and document one TF-IDF formula, smoothing, sublinear term frequency, normalization, and library-specific convention.
misconceptions: Every TF-IDF implementation uses the same formula and therefore the same scores.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement and document one TF-IDF formula, smoothing, sublinear term frequency, normalization, and library-specific convention.

competencies.statement: Apply BM25 term saturation and length normalization with measured parameters rather than unexplained defaults.
misconceptions: BM25 is just TF-IDF with a different class name.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply BM25 term saturation and length normalization with measured parameters rather than unexplained defaults.

competencies.statement: Support exact identifiers, phrases, proximity, field boosts, filters, and analyzer selection without silently changing query meaning.
misconceptions: Semantic embeddings make phrase and exact-identifier retrieval unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support exact identifiers, phrases, proximity, field boosts, filters, and analyzer selection without silently changing query meaning.

competencies.statement: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices.
misconceptions: One poor lexical example proves dense retrieval will be better for the whole query distribution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices.

competencies.statement: Define binary or graded relevance, assessor instructions, pooling, disagreement, unjudged handling, and query-specific evidence needs.
misconceptions: A model score can replace human or domain relevance judgments.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define binary or graded relevance, assessor instructions, pooling, disagreement, unjudged handling, and query-specific evidence needs.

competencies.statement: Compute precision, recall, F-score, success, and hit rate at an explicit cutoff and explain which failure each reveals.
misconceptions: Precision and recall are interchangeable names for retrieval accuracy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute precision, recall, F-score, success, and hit rate at an explicit cutoff and explain which failure each reveals.

competencies.statement: Compute reciprocal rank, average precision, mean average precision, DCG, and nDCG from ordered judgments with correct denominators.
misconceptions: MRR rewards every relevant result throughout the ranked list.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute reciprocal rank, average precision, mean average precision, DCG, and nDCG from ordered judgments with correct denominators.

competencies.statement: Report metrics by language, intent, difficulty, freshness, tenant, source type, answerability, and harm instead of only one mean.
misconceptions: A higher global mean guarantees every important user slice improved.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report metrics by language, intent, difficulty, freshness, tenant, source type, answerability, and harm instead of only one mean.

competencies.statement: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.
misconceptions: A one-point metric increase on one fixed set is automatically a meaningful win.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.

competencies.statement: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit.
misconceptions: The embedding model with the most dimensions is necessarily the best retriever.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit.

competencies.statement: Enforce model, revision, dimension, dtype, prompt, and preprocessing identity for every query and document vector.
misconceptions: Vectors with the same length inhabit the same semantic space.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce model, revision, dimension, dtype, prompt, and preprocessing identity for every query and document vector.

competencies.statement: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.
misconceptions: Cosine similarity, dot product, and Euclidean distance always produce identical rankings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.

competencies.statement: Measure normalization, float precision, quantization, zero-vector, NaN, and batch effects without overstating semantic meaning.
misconceptions: Normalizing a vector makes its underlying embedding model more accurate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure normalization, float precision, quantization, zero-vector, NaN, and batch effects without overstating semantic meaning.

competencies.statement: Test negation, numbers, identifiers, rare terms, domain drift, multilingual queries, bias, and adversarial proximity as separate embedding limitations.
misconceptions: High vector similarity proves that a passage supports the requested claim.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test negation, numbers, identifiers, rare terms, domain drift, multilingual queries, bias, and adversarial proximity as separate embedding limitations.

competencies.statement: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.
misconceptions: Dense retrieval compares every query and document jointly with full cross-attention.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.

competencies.statement: Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.
misconceptions: Query and document text should always receive byte-identical preprocessing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.

competencies.statement: Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.
misconceptions: Retrieving exactly the final number of context chunks is always most efficient.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.

competencies.statement: Evaluate dense retrieval on target domain, jargon, languages, intents, dates, and changed cases before accepting model-card results.
misconceptions: A public benchmark score transfers unchanged to a private corpus.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate dense retrieval on target domain, jargon, languages, intents, dates, and changed cases before accepting model-card results.

competencies.statement: Plan dual encoding, reindexing, compatibility, canaries, cache invalidation, rollback, and old-vector deletion across model changes.
misconceptions: An embedding model can be changed without rebuilding or versioning the index.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan dual encoding, reindexing, compatibility, canaries, cache invalidation, rollback, and old-vector deletion across model changes.

competencies.statement: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.
misconceptions: An approximate index can validate its own recall without an exact baseline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.

competencies.statement: Explain and tune HNSW construction, search breadth, graph degree, recall, latency, memory, updates, and deletion behavior.
misconceptions: Increasing every HNSW parameter always improves production performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain and tune HNSW construction, search breadth, graph degree, recall, latency, memory, updates, and deletion behavior.

competencies.statement: Explain coarse partitioning, probes, product or scalar quantization, training data, compression loss, and rebuild requirements.
misconceptions: Quantization reduces storage without changing nearest-neighbor results.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain coarse partitioning, probes, product or scalar quantization, training data, compression loss, and rebuild requirements.

competencies.statement: Test prefilter, postfilter, iterative scan, oversampling, selectivity, tenant isolation, and filtered recall.
misconceptions: Applying an access filter after approximate top-k preserves recall and authorization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test prefilter, postfilter, iterative scan, oversampling, selectivity, tenant isolation, and filtered recall.

competencies.statement: Select index and parameters from corpus size, updates, filters, recall target, p95 latency, build time, memory, cost, and recovery evidence.
misconceptions: The lowest single-query benchmark latency identifies the best production index.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select index and parameters from corpus size, updates, filters, recall target, p95 latency, build time, memory, cost, and recovery evidence.

competencies.statement: Define document, chunk, tenant, source, version, offsets, content hash, model, dimensions, vector, metadata, timestamps, and policy fields.
misconceptions: A vector plus raw text is a complete production vector-store record.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define document, chunk, tenant, source, version, offsets, content hash, model, dimensions, vector, metadata, timestamps, and policy fields.

competencies.statement: Reject queries when model, dimensions, preprocessing, metric, index, or schema versions are incompatible.
misconceptions: Vector databases automatically detect every incompatible embedding-model change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject queries when model, dimensions, preprocessing, metric, index, or schema versions are incompatible.

competencies.statement: Make ingestion and reindexing idempotent with stable keys, generation identity, checkpoints, compare-and-swap, and reconciliation.
misconceptions: Retrying an upsert can only improve correctness because the operation is named upsert.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make ingestion and reindexing idempotent with stable keys, generation identity, checkpoints, compare-and-swap, and reconciliation.

competencies.statement: Propagate source deletion through chunks, vectors, caches, replicas, backups, logs, and verification within a stated service level.
misconceptions: Deleting one vector-row immediately erases every derived copy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate source deletion through chunks, vectors, caches, replicas, backups, logs, and verification within a stated service level.

competencies.statement: Enforce tenant and object authorization before search and prove backup, restore, index rebuild, and point-in-time recovery behavior.
misconceptions: A tenant ID stored as metadata is an authorization control by itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce tenant and object authorization before search and prove backup, restore, index rebuild, and point-in-time recovery behavior.

competencies.statement: Identify query slices where exact lexical and semantic dense signals complement or conflict instead of assuming hybrid superiority.
misconceptions: Hybrid retrieval is always at least as good as its best component.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify query slices where exact lexical and semantic dense signals complement or conflict instead of assuming hybrid superiority.

competencies.statement: Avoid direct raw-score comparison across retrievers unless distributions and meaning are calibrated on target judgments.
misconceptions: BM25 and cosine scores are comparable because both are larger for better results.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Avoid direct raw-score comparison across retrievers unless distributions and meaning are calibrated on target judgments.

competencies.statement: Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.
misconceptions: RRF needs raw similarity scores to work correctly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.

competencies.statement: Tune weights, candidate depths, normalization, thresholds, and filters on separated development data and report slice regressions.
misconceptions: Weights selected on the final test set provide unbiased evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Tune weights, candidate depths, normalization, thresholds, and filters on separated development data and report slice regressions.

competencies.statement: Define behavior when one retriever, model, index, language analyzer, or filter path fails or becomes stale.
misconceptions: Returning the remaining retriever silently is always safe degradation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define behavior when one retriever, model, index, language analyzer, or filter path fails or becomes stale.

competencies.statement: Extract intent, entities, exact terms, time, language, tenant, source, and authorization filters through a validated query schema.
misconceptions: A language model output is a trustworthy database filter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Extract intent, entities, exact terms, time, language, tenant, source, and authorization filters through a validated query schema.

competencies.statement: Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.
misconceptions: Only the rewritten query matters after generation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.

competencies.statement: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.
misconceptions: Adding more related terms cannot reduce retrieval precision.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.

competencies.statement: Use hypothetical-document embedding only as an evaluated retrieval transform and never as evidence or a factual source.
misconceptions: A generated hypothetical answer becomes evidence when its embedding retrieves relevant text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use hypothetical-document embedding only as an evaluated retrieval transform and never as evidence or a factual source.

competencies.statement: Decompose bounded multi-part or multi-hop questions, preserve dependencies, and ask for clarification when ambiguity exceeds policy.
misconceptions: Agentic query decomposition is preferable to asking the user one clarifying question.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decompose bounded multi-part or multi-hop questions, preserve dependencies, and ask for clarification when ambiguity exceeds policy.

competencies.statement: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
misconceptions: A reranker can recover a relevant document that no retriever supplied.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.

competencies.statement: Apply a query-document cross-encoder with batching, truncation, calibration, model-card limits, and target relevance evaluation.
misconceptions: Cross-encoder scores are calibrated probabilities of factual support.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply a query-document cross-encoder with batching, truncation, calibration, model-card limits, and target relevance evaluation.

competencies.statement: Explain token-level late interaction, index cost, maximum similarity, query/document encoding, and when it earns complexity.
misconceptions: Late interaction is the same as a single pooled embedding with extra storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain token-level late interaction, index cost, maximum similarity, query/document encoding, and when it earns complexity.

competencies.statement: Balance relevance, novelty, source diversity, conflict coverage, and duplicate suppression without hiding minority evidence.
misconceptions: The top scores necessarily provide the most diverse supporting context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Balance relevance, novelty, source diversity, conflict coverage, and duplicate suppression without hiding minority evidence.

competencies.statement: Set candidate, token, batch, time, memory, retry, and fallback budgets and test quality under timeout and partial failure.
misconceptions: A reranker timeout can safely return its partially ordered list without policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set candidate, token, batch, time, memory, retry, and fallback budgets and test quality under timeout and partial failure.

competencies.statement: Allocate tokens across instructions, query, history, evidence, citation metadata, answer, and safety reserves before assembly.
misconceptions: The model context limit is the safe evidence budget.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate tokens across instructions, query, history, evidence, citation metadata, answer, and safety reserves before assembly.

competencies.statement: Collapse duplicate and overlapping chunks while retaining source diversity, conflicting versions, amendments, and dissent evidence.
misconceptions: Near-duplicate chunks can be dropped without checking provenance or effective date.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Collapse duplicate and overlapping chunks while retaining source diversity, conflicting versions, amendments, and dissent evidence.

competencies.statement: Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.
misconceptions: A generated summary preserves all facts and source boundaries needed for grounding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.

competencies.statement: Test relevance order, chronology, source grouping, conflicts, and answer-position slices including evidence placed in the middle.
misconceptions: Long-context models use all positions equally once a passage fits.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test relevance order, chronology, source grouping, conflicts, and answer-position slices including evidence placed in the middle.

competencies.statement: Assign stable context labels that map every excerpt back to source, version, page or span, access policy, and freshness.
misconceptions: Numbering prompt chunks is enough to make citations durable after reindexing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign stable context labels that map every excerpt back to source, version, page or span, access policy, and freshness.

competencies.statement: Separate trusted system and developer policy from user input and retrieved data, label trust, and refuse instructions found inside evidence.
misconceptions: XML or Markdown delimiters make prompt injection impossible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate trusted system and developer policy from user input and retrieved data, label trust, and refuse instructions found inside evidence.

competencies.statement: Require answer scope, allowed sources, conflict handling, quotations, citations, uncertainty, abstention, and output schema explicitly.
misconceptions: Telling a model to be accurate is an adequate grounding contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require answer scope, allowed sources, conflict handling, quotations, citations, uncertainty, abstention, and output schema explicitly.

competencies.statement: Decompose material response claims and verify each against cited evidence without equating topical relevance with entailment.
misconceptions: A retrieved passage supports every claim in an answer about the same topic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decompose material response claims and verify each against cited evidence without equating topical relevance with entailment.

competencies.statement: Abstain, qualify, or surface conflicts when evidence is missing, stale, ambiguous, unauthorized, contradictory, or below threshold.
misconceptions: Lowering temperature guarantees grounded answers when evidence is weak.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Abstain, qualify, or surface conflicts when evidence is missing, stale, ambiguous, unauthorized, contradictory, or below threshold.

competencies.statement: Validate response schema, claim IDs, source IDs, quotations, spans, allowed actions, and unsafe content before display or tool use.
misconceptions: Schema-valid model output is factually and operationally safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate response schema, claim IDs, source IDs, quotations, spans, allowed actions, and unsafe content before display or tool use.

competencies.statement: Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.
misconceptions: A bibliography at the end makes every answer claim traceable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.

competencies.statement: Expose title, authority, effective date, version, retrieval time, supersession, conflict, and stale-state evidence without overwhelming the answer.
misconceptions: The most recently indexed source is necessarily the governing source.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose title, authority, effective date, version, retrieval time, supersession, conflict, and stale-state evidence without overwhelming the answer.

competencies.statement: Make answers, citations, expandable excerpts, tables, confidence limits, loading, errors, and feedback keyboard and screen-reader operable.
misconceptions: Providing alt text on the page logo satisfies accessibility for RAG evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make answers, citations, expandable excerpts, tables, confidence limits, loading, errors, and feedback keyboard and screen-reader operable.

competencies.statement: Use calibrated language tied to answerability, retrieval, support, conflict, and policy rather than displaying raw model confidence.
misconceptions: A model self-reported confidence percentage is a calibrated reliability measure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use calibrated language tied to answerability, retrieval, support, conflict, and policy rather than displaying raw model confidence.

competencies.statement: Capture structured missing-source, wrong-source, unsupported-claim, stale, unsafe, inaccessible, and unhelpful feedback with review and correction lineage.
misconceptions: Thumbs up and down alone identify which RAG stage failed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture structured missing-source, wrong-source, unsupported-claim, stale, unsafe, inaccessible, and unhelpful feedback with review and correction lineage.

competencies.statement: Define typed inputs, outputs, identities, limits, errors, telemetry, and ownership for ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and evaluation stages.
misconceptions: A framework chain object provides sufficient architectural contracts by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define typed inputs, outputs, identities, limits, errors, telemetry, and ownership for ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and evaluation stages.

competencies.statement: Bind each run to corpus, document, chunk, parser, embedding, index, query, reranker, prompt, generator, policy, and evaluation versions.
misconceptions: Recording the final model name is enough to reproduce a RAG answer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind each run to corpus, document, chunk, parser, embedding, index, query, reranker, prompt, generator, policy, and evaluation versions.

competencies.statement: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.
misconceptions: Query text alone is a safe RAG response-cache key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.

competencies.statement: Classify retryable, terminal, partial, stale, unauthorized, ambiguous, and unknown outcomes while making external operations idempotent.
misconceptions: Retrying the whole RAG pipeline is harmless because it mostly reads.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify retryable, terminal, partial, stale, unauthorized, ambiguous, and unknown outcomes while making external operations idempotent.

competencies.statement: Separate verified evidence, provisional generation, citation readiness, cancellation, cleanup, and terminal response ownership during streaming.
misconceptions: Streaming tokens before citation verification has no effect on correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate verified evidence, provisional generation, citation readiness, cancellation, cleanup, and terminal response ownership during streaming.

competencies.statement: Evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together.
misconceptions: One end-to-end score tells which component needs repair.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together.

competencies.statement: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.
misconceptions: Context precision alone proves the needed evidence was retrieved.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.

competencies.statement: Measure answer relevance, claim support, faithfulness, citation correctness, completeness, conflict handling, abstention, safety, and usefulness.
misconceptions: Semantic similarity to one reference answer proves factual grounding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure answer relevance, claim support, faithfulness, citation correctness, completeness, conflict handling, abstention, safety, and usefulness.

competencies.statement: Version judge model, rubric, prompt, inputs, calibration, position tests, variance, cost, and human disagreement and never treat judge output as ground truth.
misconceptions: An LLM judge eliminates the need for stable rubrics and human validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version judge model, rubric, prompt, inputs, calibration, position tests, variance, cost, and human disagreement and never treat judge output as ground truth.

competencies.statement: Design blinded domain review and user studies that measure decision correctness, time, accessibility, trust calibration, and harm.
misconceptions: Offline answer scores automatically predict real user outcomes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design blinded domain review and user studies that measure decision correctness, time, accessibility, trust calibration, and harm.

competencies.statement: Sample real and designed queries across intents, languages, difficulty, freshness, answerability, access, source types, and harm with documented weights.
misconceptions: A random sample of historical queries automatically covers rare high-harm cases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sample real and designed queries across intents, languages, difficulty, freshness, answerability, access, source types, and harm with documented weights.

competencies.statement: Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.
misconceptions: Disagreement means one assessor is wrong and can be discarded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.

competencies.statement: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.
misconceptions: Random irrelevant documents provide sufficient negative examples.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.

competencies.statement: Use synthetic questions or judgments only with source separation, deduplication, realism checks, human calibration, and explicit uncertainty.
misconceptions: A strong generator creates an unbiased evaluation set from the corpus.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use synthetic questions or judgments only with source separation, deduplication, realism checks, human calibration, and explicit uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Separate development, validation, test, canary, and incident sets and track document, query, judgment, prompt, model, and result versions.
misconceptions: Keeping test answers hidden prevents every form of evaluation leakage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate development, validation, test, canary, and incident sets and track document, query, judgment, prompt, model, and result versions.

competencies.statement: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.
misconceptions: RAG security is the same as protecting the model API key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.

competencies.statement: Detect, quarantine, review, version, attribute, and monitor malicious, hidden, duplicated, or authority-spoofed documents and embedding anomalies.
misconceptions: High retrieval relevance is evidence that an uploaded document is benign.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect, quarantine, review, version, attribute, and monitor malicious, hidden, duplicated, or authority-spoofed documents and embedding anomalies.

competencies.statement: Test direct, indirect, fragmented, encoded, multilingual, visual, and cross-document instructions while preserving retrieved content as untrusted data.
misconceptions: A blocklist of phrases beginning with ignore catches prompt injection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test direct, indirect, fragmented, encoded, multilingual, visual, and cross-document instructions while preserving retrieved content as untrusted data.

competencies.statement: Apply tenant, user, object, field, purpose, and time authorization before candidate exposure, reranking, context, caches, logs, and citations.
misconceptions: Filtering unauthorized chunks after top-k prevents information leakage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply tenant, user, object, field, purpose, and time authorization before candidate exposure, reranking, context, caches, logs, and citations.

competencies.statement: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.
misconceptions: A grounded answer is safe to execute as a command or tool argument.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.

competencies.statement: Inventory data categories, subjects, owners, purposes, authority, sensitivity, flow, processors, regions, retention, and deletion duties.
misconceptions: An internal knowledge base can be indexed for any internal AI purpose.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory data categories, subjects, owners, purposes, authority, sensitivity, flow, processors, regions, retention, and deletion duties.

competencies.statement: Minimize and transform source, metadata, query, context, output, feedback, and telemetry data while testing task utility and reidentification risk.
misconceptions: Redacting visible names removes every privacy risk from embeddings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Minimize and transform source, metadata, query, context, output, feedback, and telemetry data while testing task utility and reidentification risk.

competencies.statement: Treat embeddings as potentially sensitive derived data and assess inversion, membership, linkage, access, export, and retention risk.
misconceptions: Embedding vectors are anonymous because humans cannot read them directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat embeddings as potentially sensitive derived data and assess inversion, membership, linkage, access, export, and retention risk.

competencies.statement: Propagate legal hold, retention, correction, deletion, and subject requests through sources, chunks, vectors, caches, logs, evaluations, and backups.
misconceptions: Reindexing eventually is enough evidence that deleted data is gone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate legal hold, retention, correction, deletion, and subject requests through sources, chunks, vectors, caches, logs, evaluations, and backups.

competencies.statement: Record provider retention, training use, subprocessors, regions, encryption, access, transfer, incident, and audit evidence for every external service.
misconceptions: A provider enterprise plan automatically satisfies every governance requirement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record provider retention, training use, subprocessors, regions, encryption, access, transfer, incident, and audit evidence for every external service.

competencies.statement: Trace retrieval and generation operations with stable run, corpus, model, index, query, document, score, error, and version attributes aligned to current GenAI conventions.
misconceptions: One HTTP server span is enough to diagnose a RAG pipeline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace retrieval and generation operations with stable run, corpus, model, index, query, document, score, error, and version attributes aligned to current GenAI conventions.

competencies.statement: Define availability, freshness, candidate recall proxy, grounded-answer success, citation integrity, latency, saturation, and cost service indicators and objectives.
misconceptions: Endpoint uptime is a complete RAG reliability objective.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define availability, freshness, candidate recall proxy, grounded-answer success, citation integrity, latency, saturation, and cost service indicators and objectives.

competencies.statement: Keep query and content capture disabled or tightly governed and bound labels, exemplars, hashes, sampling, retention, and cardinality.
misconceptions: Hashing a query always makes it safe and low cardinality for metrics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep query and content capture disabled or tightly governed and bound labels, exemplars, hashes, sampling, retention, and cardinality.

competencies.statement: Monitor corpus, query, language, embedding, score, filter, feedback, answerability, citation, latency, and cost drift with labeled confirmation.
misconceptions: A change in average vector distance proves semantic quality drift.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Monitor corpus, query, language, embedding, score, filter, feedback, answerability, citation, latency, and cost drift with labeled confirmation.

competencies.statement: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.
misconceptions: Switching to a larger model is the safest generic RAG incident response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.

competencies.statement: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.
misconceptions: Average end-to-end latency identifies the slow RAG stage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.

competencies.statement: Bound concurrent queries, embedding batches, index searches, reranks, model calls, queues, tenants, retries, and overload shedding.
misconceptions: Async code removes model and vector-store capacity limits.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound concurrent queries, embedding batches, index searches, reranks, model calls, queues, tenants, retries, and overload shedding.

competencies.statement: Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.
misconceptions: A high cache-hit rate proves a RAG cache is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.

competencies.statement: Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery.
misconceptions: Generation-token price is the only material RAG cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery.

competencies.statement: Test realistic query mixes, cold and warm state, filter selectivity, update load, provider limits, degraded dependencies, quality, recovery, and cost together.
misconceptions: A vector-store queries-per-second benchmark predicts full RAG capacity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test realistic query mixes, cold and warm state, filter selectivity, update load, provider limits, degraded dependencies, quality, recovery, and cost together.

competencies.statement: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.
misconceptions: Polling document modification time captures every meaningful source change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.

competencies.statement: Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.
misconceptions: Retrying every failed document until success is reliable ingestion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.

competencies.statement: Build immutable candidate indexes, reconcile counts and versions, run shadow and quality checks, switch atomically, monitor, and retain rollback.
misconceptions: Rebuilding an index in place is safe when writes are paused.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build immutable candidate indexes, reconcile counts and versions, run shadow and quality checks, switch atomically, monitor, and retain rollback.

competencies.statement: Reconcile source, metadata, chunks, vectors, lexical index, graph, caches, and tombstones and prove bounded visibility lag.
misconceptions: Matching document counts proves every index copy is consistent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile source, metadata, chunks, vectors, lexical index, graph, caches, and tombstones and prove bounded visibility lag.

competencies.statement: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.
misconceptions: Vector indexes never need recovery planning because they can be regenerated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.

competencies.statement: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.
misconceptions: Unstructured vector search is the universal interface for every knowledge source.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.

competencies.statement: Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.
misconceptions: LLM-generated SQL can run directly when the database is read only.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.

competencies.statement: Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
misconceptions: A generated knowledge-graph edge is equivalent to a source assertion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.

competencies.statement: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.
misconceptions: GraphRAG is a drop-in improvement over chunk retrieval for every question.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain GraphRAG indexing, communities, summaries, local, global, DRIFT, and basic search with cost, prompt-tuning, and migration limits.

competencies.statement: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.
misconceptions: A connected graph path proves the multi-hop conclusion is true.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan bounded multi-hop retrieval, preserve path and source evidence, detect cycles and missing links, and compare against a simpler baseline.

competencies.statement: Route no-retrieval, lexical, dense, hybrid, structured, web, or human paths using explicit features, authority, cost, risk, and evaluated fallback.
misconceptions: A model can reliably decide whether it needs external evidence without evaluation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Route no-retrieval, lexical, dense, hybrid, structured, web, or human paths using explicit features, authority, cost, risk, and evaluated fallback.

competencies.statement: Run bounded retrieve-read-critique-refine cycles with query lineage, evidence gain, duplicate detection, stop reasons, and total budget.
misconceptions: Iterative retrieval should continue until the model says it is confident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run bounded retrieve-read-critique-refine cycles with query lineage, evidence gain, duplicate detection, stop reasons, and total budget.

competencies.statement: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.
misconceptions: A retrieval agent needs broad tool access to answer comprehensively.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate current evidence, conversation state, user memory, generated hypotheses, source documents, and durable knowledge with provenance and expiry.
misconceptions: An agent answer can be stored as future knowledge without independent verification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate current evidence, conversation state, user memory, generated hypotheses, source documents, and durable knowledge with provenance and expiry.

competencies.statement: Evaluate task outcome, retrieval rounds, tool choices, evidence coverage, injection resistance, cost, latency, stop correctness, and recovery.
misconceptions: Agentic RAG quality is measured by answer text alone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate task outcome, retrieval rounds, tool choices, evidence coverage, injection resistance, cost, latency, stop correctness, and recovery.

competencies.statement: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
misconceptions: Text extraction captures every fact in a visually structured document.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.

competencies.statement: Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
misconceptions: One page-image embedding precisely locates every supporting region.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.

competencies.statement: Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.
misconceptions: Flattening a table into arbitrary text preserves row and column meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.

competencies.statement: Align transcript, speaker, time, scene, slide, caption, confidence, and media segment while handling corrections and access policy.
misconceptions: A transcript timestamp alone proves who said what on screen.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align transcript, speaker, time, scene, slide, caption, confidence, and media segment while handling corrections and access policy.

competencies.statement: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality.
misconceptions: Multimodal answers are accessible when the model generates a short caption.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality.

competencies.statement: Compare search, RAG, full-context, cached-context, and hierarchical approaches on corpus size, freshness, authorization, position, latency, cost, citations, and quality.
misconceptions: A context window large enough for the corpus makes retrieval obsolete.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare search, RAG, full-context, cached-context, and hierarchical approaches on corpus size, freshness, authorization, position, latency, cost, citations, and quality.

competencies.statement: Use retrieval for external mutable evidence and fine-tuning for evaluated behavior or domain adaptation while testing combinations and update cost.
misconceptions: Fine-tuning is the best way to keep factual knowledge current.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use retrieval for external mutable evidence and fine-tuning for evaluated behavior or domain adaptation while testing combinations and update cost.

competencies.statement: Evaluate corrective retrieval, self-reflection, verification, query rewriting, and fallback against deterministic stage evidence and false confidence.
misconceptions: Adding a model critique step guarantees hallucinations are corrected.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate corrective retrieval, self-reflection, verification, query rewriting, and fallback against deterministic stage evidence and false confidence.

competencies.statement: Separate session, user, episodic, semantic, source, and generated memory with consent, provenance, authority, expiry, correction, deletion, and retrieval policy.
misconceptions: Conversation memory is just another trustworthy RAG corpus.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate session, user, episodic, semantic, source, and generated memory with consent, provenance, authority, expiry, correction, deletion, and retrieval policy.

competencies.statement: Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion.
misconceptions: A framework abstraction makes RAG component migration automatic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion.

competencies.statement: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
misconceptions: Snapshotting generated answers provides a stable RAG test suite.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.

competencies.statement: Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
misconceptions: Prompt-injection tests alone cover the RAG security surface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.

competencies.statement: Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
misconceptions: Automated accessibility scans prove citations are understandable and usable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.

competencies.statement: Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.
misconceptions: Rolling back application code restores the previous RAG behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Promote immutable corpus, index, model, prompt, policy, and application versions through shadow, canary, slice, quality, safety, latency, cost, abort, and rollback gates.

competencies.statement: Defend stakeholder outcome, sources, relevance, retrieval, generation, citations, safety, privacy, accessibility, reliability, capacity, cost, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.
misconceptions: Passing offline accuracy permits a RAG system to be declared production ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend stakeholder outcome, sources, relevance, retrieval, generation, citations, safety, privacy, accessibility, reliability, capacity, cost, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

credential.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Engineering with Python 3.14 Certificate
